title: Later life by Christina Rossetti — Deep Poetry Lines
content:
Something this foggy day, a something which
Is neither of this fog nor of today,
Has set me dreaming of the winds that play
Past certain cliffs, along one certain beach,
And turn the topmost edge of waves to spray:
Ah pleasant pebbly strand so far away,
So out of reach while quite within my reach,
As out of reach as India or Cathay!
meaning: "Later life" by Christina Rossetti is a deep poem consisting of 14 lines. The poet explores themes of deep through vivid imagery and emotional depth. Beginning with "Something this foggy day, a something which Is neither of this fog nor of today,...", this piece captures the essence of human experience and invites reflection on the deeper currents of life and feeling. This work is part of Christina Rossetti's celebrated body of poetry that continues to resonate with readers worldwide.
